channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: In a world built for sitting, here's how to stay active — even when stuck inside
item.description: In the office, classroom and living room, working and relaxing mean sitting still. Our bodies evolved without chairs. Here are some tips for getting o...

item.title: The incidence of psychotic disorders has increased in more recent birth cohorts
item.description: 1. The incidence of psychotic disorders in Ontario has increased in more recent birth cohorts, and the age of diagnosis has decreased. Evidence Rating...

item.title: Hospitals are posting prices for patients. It's mostly industry using the data
item.description: The Trump administration pushed for price transparency in health care. But instead of patients shopping for services, it's mostly health systems and i...

item.title: Status epilepticus cause may predict likelihood of neurologic recovery but not short-term mortality
item.description: 1. The underlying cause of status epilepticus is strongly associated with the likelihood of neurologic recovery, but does not independently predict sh...

item.title: This complex brain network may explain many of Parkinson's stranger symptoms
item.description: Parkinson's disease appears to disrupt a brain network involved in everything from movement to memory.

item.title: 4 Questions Rival Lipid Panel for Assessing Men’s Heart Risk
item.description: Men in their thirties and forties often overlook key clues that could help them slow the progression of cardiovascular disease. Medscape Medical News

item.title: Investigating the use of a common vitamin to treat the aggressive brain cancer glioblastoma
item.description: Edward (Ed) Waldner had no idea why he didn't feel well, but he knew he didn't feel like himself. At 55 years of age, he felt exhausted all the time. ...

item.title: Prescribing Isotretinoin: FDA Approves iPLEDGE Modifications
item.description: Modifications to the iPLEDGE Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy aim to reduce challenges for patients, prescribers and pharmacies. Medscape Medic...

item.title: Doctors often struggle to separate professional advice from family love [PODCAST]
item.description: Subscribe to The Podcast by KevinMD. Watch on YouTube. Catch up on old episodes! Board-certified pediatrician, certified coach, and mindfulness and yo...

item.title: Polarized-light imaging shows potential for distinguishing Ehlers–Danlos subtypes
item.description: Ehlers–Danlos syndromes (EDS) are inherited conditions that affect the body's connective tissue, which provides strength and support to the skin, join...

item.title: Oral nonstatin medication significantly lowers cholesterol in high-risk patients
item.description: 1. In this randomized controlled trial, once-daily oral enlicitide was associated with sustained reductions in low-density lipoprotein cholesterol lev...

item.title: Metabolic Syndrome Tied to Glaucoma Risk
item.description: Metabolic syndrome, rather than BMI or waist circumference, is linked to an increased likelihood of glaucoma and may better identify adults who could ...

item.title: How chronic inflammation traps white blood cells in hybrid states and blocks healing
item.description: Researchers at the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill have uncovered how chronic inflammation disrupts the immune system's ability to heal th...

item.title: Remote nursing for burnout: How changing environments saved my career
item.description: For most of my nursing career, my instinct when work felt overwhelming was always the same: Look inward, fix myself, and try harder. If a shift felt c...

item.title: Rejuvenating neurons restores learning and memory in mice
item.description: Age-related memory decline and neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's are often thought of as irreversible. But the brain is not static; neurons ...

item.title: Comparing Paracetamol and Ibuprofen Safety in Infant Care
item.description: Paracetamol and ibuprofen show no significant difference in the risk for eczema or bronchiolitis during the first year of life in infants, and may be ...

item.title: Growth of spreading pancreatic cancer is fueled by 'underappreciated' epigenetic changes, shows study
item.description: In a lab-grown cell study focused on potential new treatment targets for halting the spread of most pancreatic cancers, Johns Hopkins Medicine scienti...

item.title: Cancer in Canada: Progress Made, but Challenges Remain
item.description: The latest data point to improvements for many cancer types and a continued decline in overall cancer mortality. Medscape News Canada

item.title: 2 Minute Medicine Rewind February 9, 2026

item.title: Modest 'mental exercise' can reduce risk of dementia for decades, study finds
item.description: One type of cognitive training appears to reduce the risk of dementia 20 years later.

item.title: Olympic COVID restrictions are gone, but some athletes are still self-quarantining
item.description: For most people, the pandemic days of masking are behind them. In certain corners of the Winter Olympics, though, things still look a lot like they di...

item.title: Beyond weight loss: the expanding benefits of GLP-1 receptor agonists
item.description: Glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonists (GLP-1 RAs) were introduced to help manage type 2 diabetes. They improve glycemic control and promote weight...

item.title: AI-assisted therapy: Why supervision makes the difference
item.description: Artificial intelligence in mental health has become an easy target. Headlines warn of hallucinating chatbots, reckless advice, and sensational claims ...

item.title: Medical misinformation: Navigating vaccine hesitancy with empathy
item.description: Medical misinformation is rampant but should not be overstated. Vaccination remains a good barometer of the range of opinions on a subject directly re...

item.title: An ultrasound test may more reliably detect ovarian cancer in premenopausal women than the Risk of Malignancy Index
item.description: 1. This cohort study found that the IOTA ADNEX ultrasound model at a 10% risk threshold detected more ovarian cancers in premenopausal women than the ...
